old_claim.note: Merchant Partnership Contracts provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No dependency edge remains from civic_contract_registries to merchant_partnership_contracts; partnership contracts are one possible recordable agreement class, not a prerequisite for civic record systems.

source_shape.source_locator: ASCSA description of Athenian Agora XIX: the poletai records cover public-works contracts, state mine leases, tax-collection privileges, confiscated-property sales, and public/sacred land leases.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports the registry node through civic/public records of property, leases, public contracts, tax-farming rights, and confiscated-property sales.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the source derives civic registry practice from public magistrate records and land/contract inscriptions, it does not require merchant partnership contracts as an upstream technology.

ontology_before: The graph treated merchant partnership contracts as an enabling prerequisite for civic contract registries, laundering one possible commercial contract type into the definition of a public record system.
ontology_after: Civic contract registries are scoped as public/legal record infrastructure enabled by contract law and public archives, while merchant partnership contracts remain a separate contract subtype.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the direct merchant-partnership dependency is removed. Preserved: contract-law and public-archive prerequisites still explain the legal and recordkeeping capabilities needed by the scoped registry.
would_reject_if: A source showed that Athenian or comparable civic contract registries emerged specifically from merchant partnership contracts rather than broader public legal-record practice.
The node were narrowed to merchant partnership registration only instead of public leases, property sales, public contracts, and tax-farming records.
Removing the edge made downstream nodes lose all contract-law or archive context.
short_reason: Merchant partnership contracts are recordable content, not prerequisite infrastructure for civic contract registries.
